scope_note: Harvard's official first-year application pages describe the current testing requirement and approved substitutes for exceptional access cases. Harvard does not publish per-major testing ranges here, so this panel stays school-wide and policy-focused.
policy: Harvard requires the SAT or ACT. In exceptional cases when those tests are not accessible, Harvard says applicants may instead meet the requirement with AP, IB, GCSE/A-Level, or national leaving exam results.

claims.claim: Harvard requires the SAT or ACT to meet its standardized testing requirement.

In exceptional cases when those tests are not accessible, Harvard says AP exam results, IB actual or predicted scores, GCSE/A-Level actual or predicted results, or national leaving exam results or predictions can meet the requirement.
AP or other examination results are not required, but may be submitted.
